{"key_event":"Chehabist state-building amid rising non-state mobilization","reasoning":"Chehabist reforms, the Banque du Liban, social-security institutions, and a more professional army raised administrative and coercive capacity, but weak taxation, confessional legitimacy problems, and growing Palestinian/fedayeen activity kept the state only moderately strong. Civil society remained strong through parties, unions, press, sectarian networks, and Arab-leftist mobilization, though the Deuxième Bureau and post-1961 security surveillance modestly constrained autonomous politics.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":7.2,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":6.6}
